channel.title: Dong Xing's Blog
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Dong Xing's Blog

item.title: Cartesian Tree
item.description: The Cartesian Tree is a data structure that maintains an array of n numbers a_0,a_1,\dots,a_{n-1} by maintaining a binary tree.

item.title: A Journey
item.description: MIT Informatics Tournament 2025-26 Winter Contest Dec 7, 2025 Team Even If (Dong Xing) Advanced Individual Round (High School Division): 2nd place Advanced Team Round (High School Division): 3rd place USA Computing Olympiad 2026 First Contest Jan 9, 2026 - Jan 12, 2026 Gold: 1000 points Platinum: 15...

item.title: Treap
item.description: The Treap is a data structure that maintains an array of n numbers a_0,a_1,\dots,a_{n-1} by maintaining a randomized Cartesian Tree.

item.title: Welcome to My Blog!
item.description: I am Dong Xing, a sophomore at Princeton International School of Mathematics and Science.

item.title: Fenwick Tree
item.description: The Fenwick Tree is a data structure that maintains an array of n integers a_0,a_1,\dots,a_{n-1} by maintaining s(i)=\sum_{j=i-\operatorname{lsb}(i)}^{i-1}a_i This requires \mathcal{O}(n) space.

item.title: Dijkstra's Algorithm
item.description: Dijkstra’s Algorithm is an algorithm that computes the length of the shortest path from a vertex s to every vertex in a directed graph G=(V,E) with non-negative edge weights in \mathcal{O}(|V|^2+|E|) time and \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) space, or \mathcal{O}((|V|+|E|)\log|E|) time and \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) ...

item.title: Heapsort
item.description: Heapsort is an algorithm that sorts an array of n numbers a_0,a_1,\dots,a_{n-1} in non-decreasing order in \mathcal{O}(n\log n) time and \mathcal{O}(1) space.

item.title: Prim's Algorithm
item.description: Prim’s Algorithm is an algorithm that computes the weight of the minimum spanning tree of a connected undirected graph G=(V,E) with edge weights in \mathcal{O}(|V|^2+|E|) time and \mathcal{O}(|E|) space, or \mathcal{O}(|E|\log|E|) time and \mathcal{O}(|E|) space.

item.title: Leftist Tree
item.description: The Leftist Tree is a data structure that maintains a multiset S of numbers by maintaining a binary tree.

item.title: Quickselect
item.description: Quickselect is an algorithm that finds the k-th smallest element in an array of n numbers a_0,a_1,\dots,a_{n-1} in \mathcal{O}(n) time and \mathcal{O}(n) space.
